refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation and task execution efficiency.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and outcome-focused; refining it into sub-FRs clarifies specific functional aspects that contribute to productivity, enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and verify that sub-functions contribute to meeting or exceeding the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Monitor and report productivity metrics in real-time for feedback and adjustment.

candidate_dps: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms to optimize operational parameters.
DP2: Task scheduling and dispatch system integrated with equipment control.
DP3: Real-time telemetry and reporting system for productivity monitoring.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms to optimize operational parameters.

potential_sources: Interaction between task scheduling and equipment control
Data dependency between telemetry reporting and control algorithms
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically separable but requires well-defined interfaces with DP2 and DP3 to avoid tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate sensor data for control algorithms.
Risk of suboptimal performance if task scheduling is not well integrated.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit DP decomposition for task scheduling and telemetry to reduce coupling and clarify responsibilities.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded control systems responsible for equipment operational parameters.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends operational data and productivity metrics to external monitoring modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives scheduled tasks and commands from higher-level control or teleoperation modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope; contains Vehicle but less focused containment of control logic.
High containment of control algorithms and direct equipment interfaces.
Primarily for remote control, less relevant for embedded control logic.
Focused on safety alerts, minimal containment of productivity control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Embedding adaptive control algorithms within the Vehicle module ensures tight integration with equipment hardware and minimizes interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential diffusion if control logic is duplicated in Teleoperation or NGHE Solution modules.
Risk of interface mismatch if Tasking commands are not standardized.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts for Tasking and Telemetry to prevent diffusion and ensure modular ownership.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by providing adaptive control to optimize productivity.

diffusion_risks: Possible duplication of control logic across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if not carefully managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for the selected DP, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring productivity metrics and validating control responses within the Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of adaptive control DP to Vehicle IM supports traceability and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions for Tasking and Telemetry to reduce diffusion risk.
Consider decomposing DP further to explicitly address task scheduling and telemetry.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of DP and clearer interface definitions are needed to reduce coupling and diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Monitor and report productivity metrics in real-time for feedback and adjustment.

revise.why: To explicitly separate task scheduling and telemetry functions, reducing coupling and clarifying design parameters.

assumptions: Accurate sensor data is available for adaptive control.
Tasking commands will be standardized across modules.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of control logic across modules.
evidence_needed: Performance data from adaptive control algorithms during representative operations.
Interface specification documents for Tasking and Telemetry.
